chambers.title: First Light
chambers.prompt: A glyph-line glows on the wall, its letters shifted by the hours of lost daylight. Restore the word and the dark will retreat.
hints: Each letter has slid the same distance through the alphabet.
This is a Caesar shift. Try moving every letter backward by a fixed amount.
Shift each letter back by 3: G → D, D → A …

chambers.title: The Binary Sunrise
chambers.prompt: The horizon blinks in ones and zeros. Decode the message the rising sun is sending.
hints: Light on, light off — group the blinks into bytes of 8.
Each 8-bit group is one ASCII character.

chambers.title: Logic Gate Garden
chambers.prompt: Three sun-lamps feed a gate network. Set the inputs so the final lamp (OUT) glows true. Answer like A=1,B=0,C=1.
hints: Work backward from the lamp you need lit.
OUT is an AND gate, so both X and C must be true.
X = A XOR B must be 1, and C must be 1. A=1, B=0, C=1 works.

chambers.title: Pattern of Days
chambers.prompt: The solstice calendar spirals outward: 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, ? — name the next length of day.
hints: Look at how each day relates to the days before it.
Each number is the sum of the two before it.

chambers.title: The Bombe
chambers.prompt: A tape of symbols, a handful of rules. Become the machine: run it until it halts, then read the tape.
hints: You are the machine: read the cell under the head, find the matching rule, obey it.
Each rule tells you what to write, which way to step, and your next state.
Apply the rules left to right until HALT, then read the tape: 0111.

chambers.title: The Dawn Key
chambers.prompt: Every chamber gave you a letter. Together they are the key that unlocks the morning. Decrypt the final glyph-line.
hints: The letters you collected spell the key, in the order you found them.
This is a Vigenère cipher — use your collected key word to decrypt.
Key = D, A, W, N. Decrypt GASA with it to reach the dawn.
